admin_units: All Democratic Kampuchea administrative zones / national territory
theater_description: Nationwide Democratic Kampuchea theater. Includes Phnom Penh, provincial security centres, killing fields, cooperatives, and national territory under DK control. Retained as the only full-estimate episode because the strongest direct-death and denominator sources are regime-wide.
geometry_method_note: Use the national territory of Cambodia under Democratic Kampuchea control from 1975-04-17 to 1979-01-07; note that some border areas were contested in 1977-78 but regime-wide mortality sources remain national.
military_area_note: Military exposure covers Khmer Rouge regular forces and other organized armed personnel directly subject to DK violence or fighting in Cambodia; foreign-force exposure is not comprehensively reconstructed.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure uses residents physically present in Cambodia during DK rule, with early ethnic-Vietnamese expulsion handled as a denominator adjustment rather than ignored.
split_reason: Retained because the best violent-death envelope, mass-grave evidence, and denominator anchors are national rather than subnational.
quality_note: Total direct violence is source-rich, but the civilian/military/unknown split needed for DR is mostly model-generated from mixed-status sources.

episode_name: Takeover, Phnom Penh evacuation, and early liquidation phase
theater_description: Immediate fall of Phnom Penh, forced evacuation of cities, and early killings of former Khmer Republic personnel and selected civilians.
geometry_method_note: Would require a corridor-plus-city footprint centered on Phnom Penh and major transfer routes, then a national overlay for Phase Two population transfers.
military_area_note: Would need surviving FANK and Khmer Rouge city-entry forces, not a whole-country military denominator.
civilian_area_note: Would need evacuee person-months by corridor and receiving zone; whole-country denominator would be misleading for direct evacuation mortality.
split_reason: Violence mode changes sharply at regime onset and source base is legal/case-specific, but direct and indirect evacuation deaths are hard to separate at scale.
quality_note: Good legal findings exist, but not enough clean direct-death scaling for a full DR episode.

theater_description: Operation of S-21 and other security centres, class and ethnic repression, and expanding internal purges before the terminal 1978 collapse.
geometry_method_note: National network of prisons, worksites, cooperatives, and execution sites.
military_area_note: Would need Khmer Rouge regulars plus purged internal armed cadres; many party victims remain status-ambiguous.
civilian_area_note: Civilians were exposed nationwide through cooperatives and local security systems.
split_reason: Violence mode is more stable than in 1975 and less battlefield-heavy than late 1978, but published status-separated death counts remain sparse.
quality_note: Strong qualitative and legal evidence, weak numeric status separation.

episode_name: Eastern Zone purge and DK-Vietnam war escalation
theater_description: Eastern Zone purges, Vietnamese-targeted killings, and large-scale border war culminating in the Vietnamese invasion.
geometry_method_note: Would require eastern-zone polygons plus a dynamic border-front footprint that moved materially in 1978-79.
military_area_note: Would need separate Cambodian and Vietnamese troop-exposure series; whole-country force sizes are only partial anchors.
civilian_area_note: Would need Eastern Zone and border-province civilian populations net of arrests, purges, and outflows.
split_reason: Belligerent structure and geography changed materially in 1978; sources align with an eastern-border episode.
quality_note: Strong qualitative evidence and some troop data exist, but direct civilian and military death numerators are not yet cleanly separable.
